NAME

mirror.c

SYNOPSIS

Macros


#define RAIN_UPDATE_INTERVAL_MS 100.0

Functions


EMSCRIPTEN_KEEPALIVE int mirror_init_with_args (const char *args_json)
EMSCRIPTEN_KEEPALIVE void mirror_cleanup (void)
EMSCRIPTEN_KEEPALIVE char * mirror_convert_frame (uint8_t *rgba_data, int src_width, int src_height)

#define RAIN_UPDATE_INTERVAL_MS 100.0

Definition at line 33 of file mirror.c.

Referenced by mirror_convert_frame().

Function Documentation

EMSCRIPTEN_KEEPALIVE void mirror_cleanup (void )

Definition at line 102 of file mirror.c.

102                           {
103   WASM_LOG("mirror_cleanup: START");
104   if (g_digital_rain) {
105     digital_rain_destroy(g_digital_rain);
106     g_digital_rain = NULL;
107   }
108   if (g_last_rain_output) {
109     SAFE_FREE(g_last_rain_output);
110     g_last_rain_output = NULL;
111   }
112   options_state_destroy();
113   platform_destroy();
114   WASM_LOG("mirror_cleanup: COMPLETE");
115 }




References digital_rain_destroy(),
    options_state_destroy(), platform_destroy(), SAFE_FREE,
    and WASM_LOG.






EMSCRIPTEN_KEEPALIVE
  char * mirror_convert_frame (uint8_t * rgba_data, int src_width, int
  src_height)


Definition at line 122 of file mirror.c.


122                                                                               {
123   WASM_LOG("mirror_convert_frame: START");
124   if (!rgba_data || src_width <= 0 || src_height <= 0) {
125     WASM_LOG("mirror_convert_frame: invalid args, returning NULL");
126     return NULL;
127   }
128 
129   g_frame_count++;
130   // Derive a time value from frame count using actual target FPS from options
131   int target_fps = (int)GET_OPTION(fps);
132   if (target_fps <= 0) {
133     target_fps = 60; // Default to 60 FPS if not set
134   }
135   float time_seconds = (float)g_frame_count / (float)target_fps;
136 
137   // Get current settings from options or terminal detection
138   int dst_width = (int)terminal_get_effective_width();
139   int dst_height = (int)terminal_get_effective_height();
140   color_filter_t filter = (color_filter_t)GET_OPTION(color_filter);
141   terminal_color_mode_t color_mode = (terminal_color_mode_t)GET_OPTION(color_mode);
142   if (g_frame_count <= 3 || g_frame_count % 120 == 0) {
143     log_debug("frame=%u color_filter=%d color_mode=%d", g_frame_count, (int)filter, (int)color_mode);
144   }
145   if (color_mode == TERM_COLOR_AUTO) {
146     color_mode = TERM_COLOR_TRUECOLOR; // xterm.js supports truecolor
147   }
148   palette_type_t palette_type = (palette_type_t)GET_OPTION(palette_type);
149   bool preserve_aspect_ratio = true; // Preserve webcam aspect ratio
150   bool stretch = false;              // Don't stretch - maintain proportions
151 
152   // Build terminal capabilities structure with user's color mode
153   terminal_capabilities_t caps = {0}; // Zero-initialize all fields first
154   caps.color_level = color_mode;
155   caps.capabilities = 0;
156   caps.color_count = (color_mode == TERM_COLOR_NONE)  ? 0
157                      : (color_mode == TERM_COLOR_16)  ? 16
158                      : (color_mode == TERM_COLOR_256) ? 256
159                                                       : 16777216;
160   caps.utf8_support = true;
161   caps.detection_reliable = true;
162   caps.render_mode = (render_mode_t)GET_OPTION(render_mode);
163   caps.wants_background = (caps.render_mode == RENDER_MODE_BACKGROUND);
164   caps.wants_padding = true;
165   caps.palette_type = palette_type;
166   caps.desired_fps = 60;
167   caps.color_filter = filter;
168 
169   // Convert RGBA to RGB (strip alpha channel) and optionally flip horizontally
170   rgb_pixel_t *rgb_pixels = SAFE_MALLOC(src_width * src_height * sizeof(rgb_pixel_t), rgb_pixel_t *);
171   if (!rgb_pixels) {
172     return NULL;
173   }
174 
175   bool flip_x = GET_OPTION(flip_x);
176   bool flip_y = GET_OPTION(flip_y);
177   for (int y = 0; y < src_height; y++) {
178     for (int x = 0; x < src_width; x++) {
179       int src_x = flip_x ? (src_width - 1 - x) : x;
180       int src_y = flip_y ? (src_height - 1 - y) : y;
181       int src_idx = (src_y * src_width + src_x) * 4;
182       int dst_idx = y * src_width + x;
183 
184       rgb_pixels[dst_idx].r = rgba_data[src_idx + 0];
185       rgb_pixels[dst_idx].g = rgba_data[src_idx + 1];
186       rgb_pixels[dst_idx].b = rgba_data[src_idx + 2];
187       // Alpha (rgba_data[src_idx + 3]) is discarded
188     }
189   }
190 
191   // Apply color filter to pixels if needed (except rainbow - handled in ANSI stage)
192   if (filter != COLOR_FILTER_NONE && filter != COLOR_FILTER_RAINBOW) {
193     // color_filter operates on packed RGB24 format
194     uint8_t *rgb24 = (uint8_t *)rgb_pixels;
195     int stride = src_width * 3;
196     apply_color_filter(rgb24, src_width, src_height, stride, filter, time_seconds);
197   }
198 
199   // Create image structure
200   image_t img = {.w = src_width, .h = src_height, .pixels = rgb_pixels, .alloc_method = IMAGE_ALLOC_SIMD};
201 
202   // Get palette characters based on selected palette type
203   const char *palette_chars;
204   switch (palette_type) {
205   case PALETTE_BLOCKS:
206     palette_chars = PALETTE_CHARS_BLOCKS;
207     break;
208   case PALETTE_DIGITAL:
209     palette_chars = PALETTE_CHARS_DIGITAL;
210     break;
211   case PALETTE_MINIMAL:
212     palette_chars = PALETTE_CHARS_MINIMAL;
213     break;
214   case PALETTE_COOL:
215     palette_chars = PALETTE_CHARS_COOL;
216     break;
217   case PALETTE_CUSTOM:
218     // Custom palette uses user-provided characters from options
219     palette_chars = GET_OPTION(palette_custom);
220     if (!palette_chars || palette_chars[0] == '\0') {
221       palette_chars = PALETTE_CHARS_STANDARD; // Fallback
222     }
223     break;
224   case PALETTE_STANDARD:
225   default:
226     palette_chars = PALETTE_CHARS_STANDARD;
227     break;
228   }
229 
230   // Convert to ASCII using capability-aware function
231   char *ascii_output = ascii_convert_with_capabilities(&img, dst_width, dst_height, &caps, preserve_aspect_ratio,
232                                                        stretch, palette_chars);
233 
234   // Clean up
235   SAFE_FREE(rgb_pixels);
236 
237   if (!ascii_output) {
238     log_error("ascii_convert_with_capabilities returned NULL");
239     return NULL;
240   }
241 
242   // Apply rainbow color filter to ANSI output by replacing RGB values
243   // This preserves character selection while applying rainbow colors
244   if (filter == COLOR_FILTER_RAINBOW) {
245     char *rainbow_output = rainbow_replace_ansi_colors(ascii_output, time_seconds);
246     if (rainbow_output) {
247       SAFE_FREE(ascii_output);
248       ascii_output = rainbow_output;
249     }
250   }
251 
252   bool matrix_rain = GET_OPTION(matrix_rain);
253   if (matrix_rain) {
254     // Initialize digital rain context if needed or dimensions changed
255     if (!g_digital_rain || g_digital_rain->num_columns != dst_width || g_digital_rain->num_rows != dst_height) {
256       if (g_digital_rain) {
257         digital_rain_destroy(g_digital_rain);
258       }
259       if (g_last_rain_output) {
260         SAFE_FREE(g_last_rain_output);
261         g_last_rain_output = NULL;
262       }
263       g_digital_rain = digital_rain_init(dst_width, dst_height);
264       if (!g_digital_rain) {
265         log_error("Failed to initialize digital rain effect");
266         return ascii_output; // Return without effect on error
267       }
268       g_last_rain_update_time = emscripten_get_now();
269     }
270 
271     // Update color from active filter (allows filter changes after initialization)
272     digital_rain_set_color_from_filter(g_digital_rain, filter);
273 
274     // Time-based updates: only update effect every RAIN_UPDATE_INTERVAL_MS
275     double current_time = emscripten_get_now();
276     double elapsed_ms = current_time - g_last_rain_update_time;
277 
278     if (elapsed_ms >= RAIN_UPDATE_INTERVAL_MS) {
279       // Calculate actual delta time in seconds
280       float delta_time = (float)(elapsed_ms / 1000.0);
281       g_last_rain_update_time = current_time;
282 
283       // Apply effect and cache a copy
284       char *rain_output = digital_rain_apply(g_digital_rain, ascii_output, delta_time);
285       if (rain_output) {
286         // Free old cached output
287         if (g_last_rain_output) {
288           SAFE_FREE(g_last_rain_output);
289         }
290         // Cache a copy (we'll return the original rain_output)
291         g_last_rain_output = strdup(rain_output);
292         // Return the rain output (caller will free it)
293         SAFE_FREE(ascii_output);
294         ascii_output = rain_output;
295       }
296     } else if (g_last_rain_output) {
297       // Use cached output
298       SAFE_FREE(ascii_output);
299       ascii_output = strdup(g_last_rain_output);
300     }
301   } else {
302     // Clean up digital rain context if it exists but effect is disabled
303     if (g_digital_rain) {
304       digital_rain_destroy(g_digital_rain);
305       g_digital_rain = NULL;
306     }
307     if (g_last_rain_output) {
308       SAFE_FREE(g_last_rain_output);
309       g_last_rain_output = NULL;
310     }
311     g_last_rain_update_time = 0.0;
312   }
313 
314   return ascii_output;
315 }






References apply_color_filter(),
    ascii_convert_with_capabilities(), rgb_pixel_t::b,
    terminal_capabilities_t::capabilities,
    terminal_capabilities_t::color_count,
    terminal_capabilities_t::color_filter, COLOR_FILTER_NONE,
    COLOR_FILTER_RAINBOW, terminal_capabilities_t::color_level,
    terminal_capabilities_t::desired_fps,
    terminal_capabilities_t::detection_reliable,
    digital_rain_apply(), digital_rain_destroy(),
    digital_rain_init(), digital_rain_set_color_from_filter(),
    rgb_pixel_t::g, GET_OPTION, IMAGE_ALLOC_SIMD,
    log_debug, log_error, PALETTE_BLOCKS,
    PALETTE_CHARS_BLOCKS, PALETTE_CHARS_COOL,
    PALETTE_CHARS_DIGITAL, PALETTE_CHARS_MINIMAL,
    PALETTE_CHARS_STANDARD, PALETTE_COOL, PALETTE_CUSTOM,
    PALETTE_DIGITAL, PALETTE_MINIMAL, PALETTE_STANDARD,
    terminal_capabilities_t::palette_type, rgb_pixel_t::r,
    RAIN_UPDATE_INTERVAL_MS, rainbow_replace_ansi_colors(),
    terminal_capabilities_t::render_mode, RENDER_MODE_BACKGROUND,
    SAFE_FREE, SAFE_MALLOC, TERM_COLOR_16,
    TERM_COLOR_256, TERM_COLOR_AUTO, TERM_COLOR_NONE,
    TERM_COLOR_TRUECOLOR, terminal_get_effective_height(),
    terminal_get_effective_width(),
    terminal_capabilities_t::utf8_support,
    terminal_capabilities_t::wants_background,
    terminal_capabilities_t::wants_padding, and WASM_LOG.






EMSCRIPTEN_KEEPALIVE
  void mirror_free_string (char * ptr)


Definition at line 318 of file mirror.c.


318                                    {
319   WASM_LOG("mirror_free_string: START");
320   SAFE_FREE(ptr);
321   WASM_LOG("mirror_free_string: COMPLETE");
322 }






References SAFE_FREE, and WASM_LOG.






EMSCRIPTEN_KEEPALIVE
  int mirror_init_with_args (const char * args_json)


Initialize mirror mode with command-line style arguments


Parameters


args_json JSON array of argument strings, e.g.
  ["mirror", "--width", "80",
  "--height", "40"]


Returns


0 on success, -1 on error


Definition at line 48 of file mirror.c.


48                                                  {
49   WASM_LOG("mirror_init_with_args: START");
50   WASM_LOG("mirror_init_with_args: After START log");
51 
52   // Initialize platform layer
53   WASM_LOG("Calling platform_init...");
54   WASM_LOG("mirror_init_with_args: About to call platform_init");
55   asciichat_error_t err = platform_init();
56   WASM_LOG("mirror_init_with_args: platform_init returned");
57   if (err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
58     WASM_ERROR("platform_init FAILED");
59     return -1;
60   }
61   WASM_LOG("platform_init OK");
62 
63   // Initialize logging to stderr (console.error in browser)
64   WASM_LOG("Calling log_init...");
65   log_init(NULL, LOG_DEBUG, true, false);
66   // Use debug-style format in WASM so browser console shows full log headers
67   log_set_format(OPT_LOG_TEMPLATE_DEFAULT_DEBUG, false);
68   WASM_LOG("log_init OK");
69 
70   // Parse space-separated arguments
71   WASM_LOG("Parsing arguments...");
72   char *args_copy = NULL;
73   char *argv[64] = {NULL};
74   int argc = wasm_parse_args(args_json, argv, 64, &args_copy);
75   if (argc < 0) {
76     WASM_ERROR("strdup FAILED");
77     return -1;
78   }
79   WASM_LOG_INT("Parsed arguments, argc", argc);
80 
81   // Initialize options (sets up RCU, defaults, etc.)
82   WASM_LOG("Calling options_init...");
83   err = options_init(argc, argv);
84   free(args_copy);
85 
86   if (err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
87     WASM_LOG_INT("options_init FAILED", err);
88     return -1;
89   }
90   WASM_LOG("options_init OK");
91 
92   // Initialize ANSI color code generation (dec3 cache for RGB values)
93   WASM_LOG("Calling ansi_fast_init...");
94   ansi_fast_init();
95   WASM_LOG("ansi_fast_init OK");
96 
97   WASM_LOG("mirror_init_with_args: COMPLETE");
98   return 0;
99 }






References ansi_fast_init(), ASCIICHAT_OK,
    LOG_DEBUG, log_init(), log_set_format(),
    OPT_LOG_TEMPLATE_DEFAULT_DEBUG, options_init(),
    platform_init(), WASM_ERROR, WASM_LOG,
    WASM_LOG_INT, and wasm_parse_args().
